name: Enemy Spawn Director
description: A pacing system that chooses when, where, and what enemies to spawn based on budget, pressure, safety, and encounter goals.
design_notes: A spawn director is an encounter pacing controller. It needs spawn_budget, threat budget, cooldowns, spawn group tags, player distance constraints, safe spawn validation, and pacing telemetry so pressure feels authored even when timing is dynamic.
design_purpose: Maintain encounter pressure without hand-placing every enemy wave.
Adapt enemy composition to player progress, location, party size, or current threat level.
Prevent dead air and unfair spikes by controlling spawn timing and safe placement.
player_fantasy: The player feels the encounter escalating naturally, as if the world is responding without spawning enemies unfairly in view.

parameters.name: spawn_budget
parameters.description: Budget consumed by active or queued enemies in the encounter.

parameters.name: threat_budget
parameters.description: Pressure target used to decide whether to spawn, wait, or de-escalate.

parameters.name: spawn_group_id
parameters.description: Enemy composition group selected by context and budget.

parameters.name: safe_spawn_distance_min
parameters.description: Minimum distance from player or camera for a spawn point to be eligible.

parameters.name: spawn_commit_interval_ms
parameters.description: Minimum interval between committed spawns for the same director or group.

edge_cases: All spawn points fail safe placement because they are visible, occupied, unreachable, or behind locked doors.
Spawn budget frees up at the same time multiple directors request enemies.
A player backtracks or speedruns and invalidates the intended encounter region.
Networked games need one authoritative director so clients do not spawn duplicate enemies.
common_bugs: Director spawns enemies outside navmesh or behind one-way gates because validation only checks distance.
Active enemy count desyncs when enemies die by despawn, fall out of world, or convert allegiance.
Cooldowns reset on save/load and immediately spawn a burst.
Telemetry records requested spawns but not rejected reasons, hiding why encounters feel empty.
balancing_notes: Spawn budgets should account for enemy role, travel time, player resources, and current objective pressure.
Dynamic spawning needs visible fairness rules; enemies appearing inside cleared safe zones can feel like cheating.
Track spawn reason, rejection reason, and time-to-contact to tune pacing rather than only total enemy count.
accessibility_notes: Avoid spawning threats directly behind players without readable audio, UI, or camera-safe warning cues.
Offer difficulty settings that change spawn_budget or cooldowns rather than only enemy health.

implementation_notes.unity: Keep director state in an encounter service with spawn_budget, active enemy IDs, cooldown timers, and rejection logging; validate NavMesh position, camera visibility, and path-to-player before instantiation.
implementation_notes.godot: Use spawn point nodes with tags and an Autoload or scene director that runs safe placement checks, cooldowns, and active count bookkeeping before adding enemies to the tree.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use GameMode/GameState or a dedicated subsystem for authoritative spawn decisions; EQS can validate spawn locations, while AI Debugger or Visual Logger should record director reasons.
implementation_notes.web: Represent spawn requests as deterministic events sorted by director ID and tick; validate grid reachability, player distance, and active budget before committing entities.

example_games.title: Left 4 Dead
example_games.note: Uses a director concept to pace enemy pressure.

example_games.title: Risk of Rain 2
example_games.note: Uses spawn credits and scaling pressure during runs.
